title: Maharashtrian Kanda Poha

ingredients.name: thick poha (flattened rice, medium or thick variety)
visual_checks: medium poha gives the best balance of softness and structure; thin poha turns mushy
ingredients.notes: medium poha gives the best balance of softness and structure; thin poha turns mushy

subs.name: 2 cups leftover cooked rice rinsed lightly — makes a different but equally quick dish
omit for no-onion version; add 1 boiled potato diced small for body
2 tbsp roasted cashew halves or sunflower seeds

ingredients.name: sugar
visual_checks: Maharashtrian poha always has a whisper of sweetness
ingredients.notes: Maharashtrian poha always has a whisper of sweetness

ingredients.name: sev (thin crispy noodles), for topping
visual_checks: adds a delightful crunch; add just before serving
ingredients.notes: adds a delightful crunch; add just before serving

steps.text: Rinse the poha in a colander under cold running water for 30 seconds, tossing gently. Let it drain completely — poha should be just moist enough to squeeze into a clump without dripping. Sprinkle turmeric, sugar and a pinch of salt over the drained poha and toss to coat evenly. Set aside.

Heat oil in a kadai on medium-high. Add mustard seeds and wait until they pop. Add asafoetida and curry leaves — they will crackle loudly. Immediately add the slit green chillies and sliced onion. Cook, stirring, until the onion is soft and beginning to turn translucent, about 4–5 minutes. Do not let it colour too much.

Add the roasted peanuts and stir for 30 seconds to warm them through. Reduce heat to low and add the seasoned poha. Using a spatula, gently fold everything together — do not stir vigorously or the poha grains will break. Every grain should be coated in the golden turmeric-oil mixture.

Cover and cook on low for 2 minutes, then uncover and check — the poha should be heated through and fluffed up, not wet or sticky. Sprinkle lemon juice and toss once more. Taste and adjust salt, lemon and sugar.

Serve immediately in bowls topped with fresh coriander and a few pinches of sev. Poha does not keep well once made — the texture deteriorates as it sits, so cook to order and eat right away with a cup of strong masala chai.

cross_contact_notes: Ingredient metadata cannot prove an allergen-free kitchen. Check packaged labels and cross-contact statements for your products.

substitution_notes: poha → 2 cups leftover cooked rice rinsed lightly — makes a different but equally quick dish; peanut → 2 tbsp roasted cashew halves or sunflower seeds; onion → omit for no-onion version; add 1 boiled potato diced small for body
